fields.summary: Add support for increase and decrease of container resources with resource profiles

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

status.description: This issue is being actively worked on at the moment by the assignee.
status.name: In Progress

comments.body: [~vvasudev] I am interested in working on this. Can you please add description? Can I work on this?

[~manirajv06@gmail.com] - please feel free to take it up. This is to add support for increasing and decreasing container resources, to extend the functionality added by YARN-1197 to apply it to all resource types.

[~vvasudev] Thanks.

I went through YARN-1197 discussions and was trying to understand container update flow in AMRMClientImpl using TestAMRMClient. Tried to clone TestAMRMClient#testAMRMClientWithContainerResourceChange as TestAMRMClient#testAMRMClientWithContainerResourceTypesChange and increased the container size for resource types (for example, resource1). It is working as expected. Attaching Patch containing junit test case for your reference. cc [~sunilg]/[~templedf]
[~manirajv06@gmail.com] We have to extend from 2 resources to all resources basedon the changes in YARN-1197. I can see only test case changes here? Did u miss some other files?

[~sunilg] Thanks.

While trying to understand https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-5592, got an impression that there is an support for resource types already available at the core level. So made changes in test cases only just to know whether increase/decrease containers size even for resource types works or not. It worked and felt that it is already meeting this JIRA requirements. Am I missing anything here?

Had a offline discussion with [~sunilg] about the next steps. Summary is to add more test cases in addition to earlier patch (for example, increasing and decreasing the same container etc) to ensure this feature works even for resource types without any issues. Added patch for the same. Also YARN-4175 is still open and can help us in this context.

status.description: A patch for this issue has been uploaded to JIRA by a contributor.
status.name: Patch Available

comments.body: [~vvasudev] I am interested in working on this. Can you please add description? Can I work on this?

[~manirajv06@gmail.com] - please feel free to take it up. This is to add support for increasing and decreasing container resources, to extend the functionality added by YARN-1197 to apply it to all resource types.

[~vvasudev] Thanks.

I went through YARN-1197 discussions and was trying to understand container update flow in AMRMClientImpl using TestAMRMClient. Tried to clone TestAMRMClient#testAMRMClientWithContainerResourceChange as TestAMRMClient#testAMRMClientWithContainerResourceTypesChange and increased the container size for resource types (for example, resource1). It is working as expected. Attaching Patch containing junit test case for your reference. cc [~sunilg]/[~templedf]
[~manirajv06@gmail.com] We have to extend from 2 resources to all resources basedon the changes in YARN-1197. I can see only test case changes here? Did u miss some other files?

[~sunilg] Thanks.

While trying to understand https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-5592, got an impression that there is an support for resource types already available at the core level. So made changes in test cases only just to know whether increase/decrease containers size even for resource types works or not. It worked and felt that it is already meeting this JIRA requirements. Am I missing anything here?

Had a offline discussion with [~sunilg] about the next steps. Summary is to add more test cases in addition to earlier patch (for example, increasing and decreasing the same container etc) to ensure this feature works even for resource types without any issues. Added patch for the same. Also YARN-4175 is still open and can help us in this context.
